title: Contested legitimacy of the February 2026 national election
summary: A recurring storyline disputing the fairness of the 13th parliamentary election through fabricated claims of rigging, postponement, recounts and forged foreign verdicts.
why_it_matters: Manufactured doubt about the vote's integrity can delegitimise the incoming government and trigger unrest; forged UN/US/EC statements are hard for ordinary voters to verify.

title: Forged news photocards fabricating quotes by advisers and public figures
summary: A high-volume technique in which forged photocards impersonating major outlets put invented statements into the mouths of interim-government advisers, officials and celebrities.
why_it_matters: Impersonating trusted news brands weaponises their credibility, erodes trust in real journalism and can trigger political flashpoints before a correction reaches the public.

title: Sharif Osman Hadi killing weaponised into political-violence disinformation
summary: After the December 2025 shooting of Inqilab Mancha spokesperson Sharif Osman Hadi, a coordinated wave of fabricated photocards, AI images and misattributed videos sought to pin blame on rival groups.

title: Indian media amplification of a Hindu-persecution framing of Bangladesh
summary: Indian outlets, BJP-linked figures and social accounts repeatedly frame Bangladesh as engulfed in anti-Hindu violence, often using debunked or out-of-context material.
why_it_matters: Sustained foreign framing of systematic minority persecution shapes international perception, pressures Dhaka diplomatically, and feeds domestic communal tension.
